skill.name: higgsfield-generate
skill.description:
Generate images/videos via Higgsfield AI. Default: GPT
Image 2 for images/design/text, Seedance 2.0 for video,
Nano Banana 2/Pro for character/reference image work,
Marketing Studio for ads with avatars/products/hooks,
settings, plus Soul V2/Cinema/Cast/Location and Kling
3.0. Use when: "generate an image", "make a video",
"animate this photo", "image-to-video",
"edit/stylize/remix this image", "produce a clip",
"create an ad", "make a UGC video", "product demo",
"unboxing", "brand video", "presenter video",
"import product from URL", "create avatar for ad",
or "analyze video virality". Supports image-to-image,
image-to-video, references, job/upload IDs, and
Marketing Studio. Chain with higgsfield-soul-id for
face/identity consistency. Virality Predictor
(`brain_activity`) analyzes video virality: hook strength,
attention, retention, distraction risk, and creative
score. NOT for: Soul Character training (use
higgsfield-soul-id), product photoshoots, marketplace
listing cards, text/chat/TTS tasks.
skill.summary: Generate images, videos, and ads via the Higgsfield AI platform using 30+ models through simple conversation.
skill.author: higgsfield-ai

security_audit.summary: All 849 static findings are false positives in markdown documentation files showing CLI command examples inside fenced code blocks, not executable code. The only noteworthy pattern is the curl-to-sh install command (SKILL.md:39), which is the standard documented installation method for the higgsfield CLI and uses a hardcoded URL to the project's own GitHub. The skill is a legitimate CLI wrapper for the Higgsfield AI platform and presents minimal security risk.

low_findings.title: Curl-to-shell installation pattern
low_findings.description: SKILL.md:39 uses curl piped to sh for CLI installation. This is the standard documented install method for the higgsfield CLI with a hardcoded URL to the project's own GitHub repository. Not a security threat but follows a pattern that could be risky if the source were compromised.

content.user_title: Generate Images, Videos, and Ads with AI
content.value_statement: Creating professional AI-generated images and videos typically requires complex tooling and prompt engineering. This skill removes that complexity by connecting Claude directly to the Higgsfield AI platform, letting you generate, edit, and analyze visual content through simple conversation.
actual_capabilities: Generate high-fidelity images using GPT Image 2, Nano Banana, Soul, and other AI models
Create videos with Seedance 2.0, Kling, Marketing Studio, and Cinema Studio video models
Produce branded marketing ads with avatars, products, hooks, and brand kits
Analyze video virality using the Virality Predictor for hook strength and attention scoring
Transform images through image-to-image editing and image-to-video animation
Import products from URLs and create custom avatars for advertising campaigns
limitations: Requires an active Higgsfield account with available credits for API usage
Cannot train Soul Characters (use the higgsfield-soul-id skill for character training)
Does not perform text generation, chat, or text-to-speech tasks
Model availability and parameters depend on the Higgsfield API live catalog

use_cases.title: Marketing content creator
use_cases.description: Generate branded UGC videos, product demos, and social media ads with custom avatars and products
use_cases.target_user: Social media managers and marketing teams

use_cases.title: Video content producer
use_cases.description: Create cinematic video content, animate still images, and analyze video performance for virality potential

use_cases.title: E-commerce brand manager
use_cases.description: Import products from URLs, generate ad creatives, and produce product showcase videos for online stores

prompt_templates.title: Basic image generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate an image of a red fox in a snowy forest at golden hour, cinematic style
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick image creation with default settings

prompt_templates.title: Video from image
prompt_templates.prompt: Animate this photo into a 5-second video with a slow dolly-in effect
prompt_templates.scenario: Converting a static image to video with motion

prompt_templates.title: Marketing ad video
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a 15-second UGC product review video for our organic coffee brand, featuring a presenter avatar
prompt_templates.scenario: Creating a branded advertising video with Marketing Studio

prompt_templates.title: Video virality analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this video for hook strength, attention retention, and overall creative score
prompt_templates.scenario: Evaluating a finished video performance potential

output_examples.input: Generate an image of a modern living room with natural lighting and Scandinavian design
output_examples.output: A URL to the generated image asset

output_examples.input: Create a 10-second brand video showcasing our new running shoes with a presenter
output_examples.output: A URL to the generated video asset and a summary of the output quality

best_practices: Use concrete sensory prompts that describe subject, setting, and style for the best results
Start with the recommended default model for each task type and upgrade only when quality needs improvement
Always use the --wait flag to let the CLI handle polling and return results automatically
anti_patterns: Do not invent model names - always run higgsfield model list to see the live catalog
Do not pass prompt-only models reference media files as they will reject the submission
Do not attempt to train Soul Characters through this skill - use higgsfield-soul-id instead

faq.question: What AI models does this skill support?
faq.answer: It supports over 30 models for image generation, video generation, and video analysis. The full live catalog is available through the higgsfield CLI.

faq.question: Do I need a Higgsfield account?
faq.answer: Yes, you need an active Higgsfield account with available credits. The CLI handles authentication through device-flow login.

faq.question: Can I use my own images or videos as input?
faq.answer: Yes, you can provide local image and video files as input. The CLI auto-uploads them before submission.

faq.question: What is the difference between GPT Image 2 and Nano Banana?
faq.answer: GPT Image 2 is best for general high-fidelity image generation and text rendering. Nano Banana is optimized for character and cartoon-style work.

faq.question: Can this skill train a custom character identity?
faq.answer: No, Soul Character training is done through the higgsfield-soul-id skill. This skill consumes the trained character reference ID for generation.

faq.question: What is the Virality Predictor?
faq.answer: It is a video analysis tool that scores a clip on hook strength, attention retention, distraction risk, and creative quality. It returns a detailed text report with an Open report link.
